an10.96:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Ānanda was staying near Rājagaha in Tapodā monastery.
an10.96:1.2: Then, as night was ending, he got up & went to the Tapodā Hot Springs to bathe his limbs.
an10.96:1.3: Having bathed his limbs and having gotten out of the springs, he stood wearing only his lower robe, drying his limbs.
an10.96:1.4: Kokanuda the wanderer, as night was ending, also got up & went to the Tapodā Hot Springs to bathe his limbs.
an10.96:2.1: He saw Ven. Ānanda from afar,
an10.96:2.2: and on seeing him said to him,
an10.96:4.1: “I would like to ask you about a certain point, if you would give me leave to pose a question.”
an10.96:4.2: “Go ahead and ask. Having heard (your question), I’ll inform you.”
an10.96:5.1: “How is it, my friend: ‘The cosmos is eternal. Only this is true; anything otherwise is worthless.’ Is this the sort of view you have?”
an10.96:5.2: “No, my friend, I don’t have that sort of view.”
an10.96:6.1: “Very well, then: ‘The cosmos is not eternal. Only this is true; anything otherwise is worthless.’ Is this the sort of view you have?”
an10.96:6.2: “No, my friend, I don’t have that sort of view.”
an10.96:7.3: ‘The soul & the body are the same … ’ …
an10.96:7.4: ‘The soul is one thing and the body another … ’ …
an10.96:7.6: ‘After death a Tathāgata does not exist … ’ …
an10.96:7.7: ‘After death a Tathāgata both does & does not exist … ’ …
an10.96:7.8: ‘After death a Tathāgata neither does nor does not exist. Only this is true; anything otherwise is worthless.’ Is this the sort of view you have?”
an10.96:7.9: “No, my friend, I don’t have that sort of view.”
an10.96:8.1: “Then in that case, do you not know or see?”
an10.96:8.2: “No, my friend. It’s not the case that I don’t know, I don’t see. I do know. I do see.”
an10.96:9.1: “But on being asked, ‘How is it, my friend: “The cosmos is eternal. Only this is true; anything otherwise is worthless.”
an10.96:9.2: Is this the sort of view you have?’ you inform me,
an10.96:9.3: ‘No, my friend, I don’t have that sort of view.’
an10.96:10.1: On being asked, ‘Very well then: “The cosmos is not eternal … ” …
an10.96:11.3: “The soul & the body are the same … ” …
an10.96:11.4: “The soul is one thing and the body another … ” …
an10.96:11.6: “After death a Tathāgata does not exist … ” …
an10.96:11.7: “After death a Tathāgata both does & does not exist … ” …
an10.96:11.8: “After death a Tathāgata neither does nor does not exist. Only this is true; anything otherwise is worthless.” Is this the sort of view you have?’ you inform me, ‘No, my friend, I don’t have that sort of view.’
an10.96:12.1: But on being asked, ‘Then in that case, do you not know or see?’ you inform me,
an10.96:12.2: ‘No, my friend. It’s not the case that I don’t know or see.
an10.96:12.3: I do know. I do see.’
an10.96:12.4: Now, how is the meaning of this statement to be understood?”
an10.96:13.1: “‘The cosmos is eternal. Only this is true; anything otherwise is worthless,’ is a viewpoint.
an10.96:13.5: ‘The soul & the body are the same … ’ …
an10.96:13.6: ‘The soul is one thing and the body another … ’ …
an10.96:13.8: ‘After death a Tathāgata does not exist … ’ …
an10.96:13.9: ‘After death a Tathāgata both does & does not exist … ’ …
an10.96:13.10: ‘After death a Tathāgata neither does nor does not exist. Only this is true; anything otherwise is worthless,’ is a viewpoint.
an10.96:14.1: The extent to which there are viewpoints, view-stances, the taking up of views, obsessions with views, the cause of views, & the uprooting of views: that’s what I know. That’s what I see.
an10.96:14.2: Knowing that, I say ‘I know.’ Seeing that, I say ‘I see.’
an10.96:14.3: Why should I say ‘I don’t know, I don’t see’?
an10.96:14.4: I do know. I do see.”
an10.96:15.1: “What is your name, my friend? What do your companions in the holy life call you?”
an10.96:15.3: and that’s what my companions in the holy life call me.”
an10.96:15.4: “What? Have I been talking with the great teacher without realizing that he was
an10.96:15.7: he was Ven. Ānanda, I would not have cross-examined him so much.
an10.96:15.8: May Ven. Ānanda please forgive me.”
